payload.value:
Discovered 2026-05-10 across two sources. Two display strings invert the natural-English reading of dep direction:

1. act dep add A B --type blocks (where A is blocked by B) prints: 'Edge A --[blocks]--> B already present'. Reads as 'A blocks B', means the opposite.
2. act show A on a blocked issue prints: 'dep: blocks <blocker-id>'. Reads as 'A blocks <blocker-id>', means 'A is blocked by <blocker-id>'.

This caused a fresh UX-eval agent to incorrectly conclude that act ready was buggy — they assumed the natural-English reading and were surprised when ready hid the issue they thought was the blocker. (ready is in fact correct per the canonical 'A is blocked by B' semantics; the display strings just don't reflect that.)

Took the bootstrap session a full minute and an act show + ready cross-check to convince myself the deps were correct. An agent without that option is going to file deps wrong half the time. Compounding fix: dep add --help and act help workflow should include a one-line direction primer ('dep add A B --type blocks: A is blocked by B; A is hidden from ready until B closes').

Related to act-63a1 (directional aliases on input). This one is about output display.
